title: Defining Relative Clauses
description: Defining relative clauses identify which person or thing you mean. No commas.
summary_pt_br: Orações adjetivas restritivas: WHO/WHICH/THAT/WHERE/WHOSE. Sem vírgula. Não pode tirar sem perder o sentido.

scenes.subtitle_en: Defining relative clauses identify which person or thing you mean. No commas.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Orações adjetivas restritivas: WHO/WHICH/THAT/WHERE/WHOSE. Sem vírgula. Não pode tirar sem perder o sentido.

scenes.text_en: Defining relative clauses identify which person or thing you mean. No commas. Today's focus: defining relative clauses. The student who scored the highest was from Brazil.
scenes.text_pt: Orações adjetivas restritivas: WHO/WHICH/THAT/WHERE/WHOSE. Sem vírgula. Não pode tirar sem perder o sentido. Foco de hoje: defining relative clauses. O aluno que tirou a nota mais alta era do Brasil.

scenes.title: Defining Relative Clauses
scenes.body_en: Use WHO for people, WHICH for things, THAT for both, WHERE for places, WHOSE for possession. 'The teacher who helped me is Zara.' The clause is essential — remove it and the sentence loses meaning.
scenes.body_pt: Orações adjetivas restritivas: WHO/WHICH/THAT/WHERE/WHOSE. Sem vírgula. Não pode tirar sem perder o sentido.

examples.en: The student who scored the highest was from Brazil.
examples.pt: O aluno que tirou a nota mais alta era do Brasil.

examples.en: This is the café where I always meet my friends.
examples.pt: Este é o café onde sempre encontro meus amigos.

examples.en: I know a woman whose son plays in the national team.
examples.pt: Conheço uma mulher cujo filho joga na seleção.

exercise.question: Complete: "The ___ who scored the highest was from Brazil."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "The student who scored the highest was from Brazil."
exercise.hint_pt_br: O aluno que tirou a nota mais alta era do Brasil.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
This is the café where I always meet my friends.
exercise.explanation: The correct sentence is: "This is the café where I always meet my friends."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Este é o café onde sempre encontro meus amigos.

exercise.explanation: Five key terms from the lesson. Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

scenes.title: Defining Relative Clauses — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre defining relative clauses. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Tell me about one teacher who really influenced your life.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: It was the teacher who taught me to read English.
turns.pt_hint: Que me ensinou a ler inglês.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: What was the school where you met her?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: It was the small school that is near my grandmother's house.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Do you remember the classroom where she taught?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Yes, the one whose walls were full of posters.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: What was the first book which she made you read?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: It was the novel that I still keep on my shelf.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Is there a student who reminds you of yourself at that age?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Yes, the girl who sits in the front row.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Beautiful. The best teachers plant seeds that grow forever.

scenes.title: The teacher who changed a life
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, detalhes e sua opinião. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Describe the teacher, the school, and one lesson that stayed with the speaker. Use at least two relative clauses.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Resumo curto com quem falou, o que disseram e sua opinião.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In a small classroom by the coast, a conversation unfolded about the power of a single teacher.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: I remember the professor who first believed in my potential when I was only fifteen.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: He taught in a school whose library had only a few hundred books, but he made each one feel special.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: Do you remember a specific lesson that changed how you see the world?

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: Yes, the lesson that taught me that curiosity matters more than memorization.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Years later, Kumar still passed those same values to every student who walked into his school.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Defining Relative Clauses". Defining relative clauses identify which person or thing you mean. No commas. The student who scored the highest was from Brazil. This is the café where I always meet my friends. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: Prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Defining Relative Clauses". I liked it because it was clear and useful. See you next time!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule from last year
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Defining Relative Clauses".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para continuar praticando. Salve os links e volte sempre.
